title: advance_diversifier_to uses no transaction; next_diversifier does
description: Scenario: next_diversifier wraps its read-modify-write in a SQLite transaction (self.pool.begin()...tx.commit()). advance_diversifier_to does two separate queries with an await between them. With max_connections=1 this is safe because no other task can issue queries concurrently at the SQLite level. But the asymmetry is a footgun: if max_connections is ever raised (or if a second wallet task is added), advance_diversifier_to has a TOCTOU between its SELECT and INSERT. The inconsistency is documented in the code comment ('If concurrent wallet access is ever added, this will need a BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction'), which prevents silent breakage, but the inconsistent pattern will confuse a future contributor who models advance_diversifier_to on next_diversifier.
